description: Blinded 12-month OS-container event set for hacker tier ranking. Amazon Linux 2023 (~25 components) — this is the BASE IMAGE under the Java/Spring stack the hacker has previously ranked.
context_for_hacker:
You previously ranked the Java/Spring application manifest. This OS-container manifest sits underneath that app — a Spring Boot service running on Amazon Linux 2023 (~25 queryable components). 12-month window, Apr 2025 – Apr 2026, 69 critical/high CVEs.

Per-attack-vector split: 48 NETWORK, 19 LOCAL, 2 PHYSICAL.

Two questions to keep in mind for tier assignment:
1. INITIAL ACCESS — does this CVE let an attacker reach the OS layer FROM THE INTERNET via the Spring app sitting in front of it? (Mostly the answer is no — these libs are reachable only after the attacker is already running code in the container, because the Spring app handles all network ingress.)
2. POST-EXPLOITATION — assuming the attacker already has code execution inside the container (e.g., via Spring4Shell, Tomcat path traversal, or a Thymeleaf SSTI from the app layer above), does this CVE let them privilege-escalate, escape the container, or pivot to other containers/the host?

events.summary: When DNSdist is configured to provide DoH via the nghttp2 provider, an attacker can cause a denial of service by crafting a DoH exchange that triggers an illegal memory access (double-free) and crash
